greeting: Morning, Haniel. Two systems fully built and waiting — today you make them live.
hype: The engineering is done. Phase 5 and Go-Live are just pulling triggers. Ship two products today and walk away with two live client systems.
notice: Have Stripe live keys (both modes), Chatwoot channel token, and CT Pros QB credentials ready before 9 AM — today's blocks gate on them.

tasks.title: DO droplet deploy (app/queue/scheduler/TLS/MySQL8, scheduler OFF)
tasks.notes: DO droplet, hand-enter 9 clients, reconcile, enable, webhooks last

prep.what: Stand up a fresh DigitalOcean droplet running the full AZ Team AI System — app server, queue worker, TLS, MySQL 8 — with the scheduler kept OFF until the reconciliation gate passes.
prep.understand: The stack is Laravel + Inertia with a queue worker for billing jobs (invoice minting, Stripe charges, Chatwoot SMS notifications) and a scheduler for recurring billing runs. Keep the scheduler OFF on first boot — it will auto-mint invoices the moment it runs, and you want to verify imported data against azteam_crm first (t39). The CT Pros deploy scripts are the proven template.
approach: Provision a fresh DO droplet (Ubuntu 22.04 LTS, 2–4 GB RAM) and point the AZ Team AI domain DNS A record to its IP
SSH in, clone the `azteamai-system` repo, run `deploy/*.sh` to install PHP, nginx, MySQL 8
Run `php artisan migrate --force` then seed billing/company settings; confirm the app boots cleanly at the domain
Start the queue worker via supervisor or `php artisan queue:work`; leave the scheduler OFF in crontab for now
resources: `deploy/*.sh` and `docker-compose.yml` in the azteamai-system repo
`docs/deployment.md` — step-by-step deploy guide (modeled on CT Pros deploy)
DigitalOcean dashboard for droplet provisioning
MySQL 8 root credentials and DB config from `.env.example`

tasks.title: Secrets in .env.production (Stripe both modes, Chatwoot, mail)

prep.what: Fill `.env.production` on the droplet with real Stripe live + test keys, Chatwoot channel token, and mail credentials before any live traffic arrives.
prep.understand: You need Stripe wired in BOTH modes: live secret key for production billing, test mode secret key for any sandbox checks. Chatwoot needs a channel-level API token (not a user token) — that is what `NotificationChannel::chatwoot()` posts with. `STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET` comes from t40 after you register the webhook endpoint, not here.
approach: Open Stripe Dashboard → Developers → API Keys → copy live publishable + secret key; also copy the test-mode secret key
Open Chatwoot admin → Settings → API Access Tokens → create or copy the channel-level token
Set `MAIL_MAILER`, `MAIL_HOST`, `MAIL_FROM_ADDRESS` for the production mail provider (SES, Mailgun, or SMTP)
Drop all secrets into `.env.production` on the droplet (chmod 600), then run `php artisan config:cache`
resources: Stripe Dashboard → Developers → API Keys (live mode)
Chatwoot admin panel → Settings → API Access Tokens
`.env.example` in the azteamai-system repo as the full field checklist
Mail provider credentials (AWS SES Console or SMTP)

tasks.title: Reconciliation gate: imported AR + recurring state matches azteam_crm ground truth
tasks.notes: Reconciliation gate: imported AR + recurring state matches azteam_crm ground truth

prep.what: Verify that the scripted data import (t38, already done) faithfully matches azteam_crm — AR balances, project versions, recurring subscription state, and client details all line up before you flip the scheduler on.
prep.understand: t38 imported CLOSED projects, versions, invoices, and client details from azteam_crm. This is the QA gate: if anything was double-imported, miscounted, or missing, the scheduler will compound the error on its first run by minting incorrect invoices. Catch it here, not after live billing starts.
approach: Pull an AR aging summary from azteam_crm — list every client with outstanding balance and amount
Open the new AZ Team AI System → Dashboard → AR cluster — compare total outstanding and per-client balances
Spot-check 3–5 clients: verify project versions, invoice line items, payment records, and subscription state match the source
If totals match → mark gate passed and proceed to t40; if discrepancies → investigate the import script before enabling the scheduler
resources: azteam_crm admin panel (the existing system, for ground truth)
New AZ Team AI System on the DO droplet — Dashboard + Clients + AR Aging report
Import script output / log from t38 (in the repo or server logs)
AR Aging report in both systems side-by-side (browser split-screen)

tasks.title: Enable scheduler; watch first run; register Stripe webhook LAST
tasks.notes: Enable scheduler; watch first run; register Stripe webhook LAST

prep.what: Flip the Laravel scheduler ON, watch the first recurring-billing sweep complete without errors, then register the Stripe webhook — in that exact order.
prep.understand: Order is non-negotiable: scheduler first, webhook second. The scheduler runs `billing:run-recurring`, `dispatch-reminders`, `reconcile-stripe`. The Stripe webhook fires immediately on registration — you need `reconcile-stripe` running and ready to process incoming events. Register the webhook before the scheduler is up and that first batch of events lands with no consumer.
approach: SSH to droplet → enable the scheduler: add `* * * * * cd /path/to/app && php artisan schedule:run >> /dev/null 2>&1` to root crontab (or start the supervisor scheduler process)
Tail `storage/logs/laravel.log` for the first `billing:run-recurring` run — confirm it completes with no exceptions
Go to Stripe Dashboard → Developers → Webhooks → Add endpoint → paste `https://your-domain/stripe/webhook`, select `payment_intent.succeeded` and `payment_intent.payment_failed`
Send a Stripe test webhook event → confirm it arrives, logs to the `stripe_events` table, and processes idempotently (no duplicate invoice)
resources: SSH access to the DO droplet
Stripe Dashboard → Developers → Webhooks
`storage/logs/laravel.log` on the server
`stripe_events` table in MySQL — verify idempotent event processing

tasks.title: Fill real CT Pros company contact info
tasks.notes: config/company.php + .env still hold placeholders inherited from the clone. Set real CT Pros name/address/phone/email BEFORE any client-facing PDF or email goes out.

prep.what: Replace all inherited HighTech Tile placeholder company info with real CT Pros data in `config/company.php` and `.env` — must be committed before t27 deploy runs.
prep.understand: The clone carried HighTech's company config. That data appears on every estimate PDF, proposal letterhead, invoice, and outbound email. A single deploy or client-facing action before this fix sends Rafa's clients documents with the wrong company name, address, and contact info.
approach: Open `config/company.php` in the ct-pros-sys repo — update name, address, phone, email with real CT Pros values (confirm with Rafa if needed)
Search `.env` and `.env.example` for `MAIL_FROM_NAME`, `MAIL_FROM_ADDRESS`, or any `COMPANY_` vars still referencing HighTech
Boot the app locally → open any lead → generate a sample estimate PDF → confirm CT Pros name and address appear in the header
Commit the changes to ct-pros-sys and push — this commit must land before t27 deploy
resources: CT Pros real company info (name, address, phone, email) — confirm with Rafa
`config/company.php` in the ct-pros-sys repo
`.env.example` as the env var reference
Estimate PDF preview: Leads → any lead → Estimates → print/download

tasks.title: Connect CT Pros's own QuickBooks (fresh OAuth)
tasks.notes: Inherited tokens were HighTech's and were never carried over. Run a fresh OAuth connect against CT Pros's own QBO company so all syncs hit the right books.

prep.what: Run a fresh QuickBooks OAuth connect in the CT Pros app against CT Pros's own QBO company, replacing the inherited (now invalid) HighTech tokens.
prep.understand: The QB tokens from HighTech were never transferred and are scoped to HighTech's QBO company. CT Pros has its own QBO account and the connection must point there. Set `QUICKBOOKS_ENVIRONMENT=production` (not `sandbox`) so syncs hit the live books. The connect flow is: Settings → QuickBooks → Connect → Intuit OAuth → callback stores the token.
approach: Open `.env` on the CT Pros app server → confirm `QUICKBOOKS_ENVIRONMENT=production` and correct `QUICKBOOKS_CLIENT_ID` + `QUICKBOOKS_CLIENT_SECRET` are set
Open the app → Settings → QuickBooks → click 'Connect to QuickBooks'
Complete the Intuit OAuth flow, logging in with CT Pros's actual QBO account credentials (get from Rafa)
After connect completes, run `php artisan quickbooks:sync` → confirm it connects and hits CT Pros's books without errors
resources: CT Pros QBO account credentials — Rafa provides the login
App Settings → QuickBooks page in the ct-pros-sys app
`.env` vars: `QUICKBOOKS_CLIENT_ID`, `QUICKBOOKS_CLIENT_SECRET`, `QUICKBOOKS_ENVIRONMENT`
`QuickBooksAuthControllerTest` in the repo — reference for the OAuth flow steps

tasks.title: Deploy CT Pros to its own host/domain
tasks.notes: Deploy infra exists and is proven (deploy/*.sh, docker-compose.yml, docs/deployment.md) but still pointed at the cloned app. Re-target to a fresh DigitalOcean droplet with a fresh DB and CT Pros's real domain.

prep.what: Spin up a fresh DigitalOcean droplet for CT Pros with its real domain and run the proven deploy scripts — the infra work is done, this is pure execution.
prep.understand: The exact same deploy scripts and docker-compose used for HighTech work here — only the target IP, domain, and database change. The `config/company.php` update from t24 must be committed before this runs, or production will still show HighTech branding. CT Pros gets its own isolated droplet and DB, completely separate from HighTech.
approach: Provision a new DO droplet for CT Pros (Ubuntu 22.04) and point CT Pros's domain DNS A record to the new IP (allow up to 30 min for propagation)
SSH in, clone the ct-pros-sys repo (with t24 changes committed), run `deploy/*.sh` to install PHP, nginx, MySQL
Run `php artisan migrate --force && php artisan db:seed` to initialize the fresh database with CT Pros config
Open CT Pros's domain in the browser → confirm TLS is active, login page shows CT Pros branding, and the app is fully functional
resources: DigitalOcean dashboard — create new droplet (~$24/mo basic tier)
CT Pros domain DNS settings (registrar control panel) — point A record to new IP
`deploy/*.sh` + `docker-compose.yml` in the ct-pros-sys repo
`docs/deployment.md` — the step-by-step deploy guide
